segments.text: Then Agrippa said unto Paul,
Thou art permitted to speak for thyself.
segments.speaker: Agrippa

segments.text: Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:
I think myself happy, king Agrippa, because I shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof I am accused of the Jews:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Especially because I know thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews: wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: My manner of life from my youth, which was at the first among mine own nation at Jerusalem, know all the Jews;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Which knew me from the beginning, if they would testify, that after the most straitest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Unto which promise our twelve tribes, instantly serving God day and night, hope to come. For which hope's sake, king Agrippa, I am accused of the Jews.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Why should it be thought a thing incredible with you, that God should raise the dead?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I verily thought with myself, that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Which thing I also did in Jerusalem: and many of the saints did I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I gave my voice against them.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I punished them oft in every synagogue, and compelled them to blaspheme; and being exceedingly mad against them, I persecuted them even unto strange cities.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Whereupon as I went to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: At midday, O king, I saw in the way a light from heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me and them which journeyed with me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I said,
Who art thou, Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And he said,
Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But shewed first unto them of Damascus, and at Jerusalem, and throughout all the coasts of Judæa, and then to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For these causes the Jews caught me in the temple, and went about to kill me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Having therefore obtained help of God, I continue unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying none other things than those which the prophets and Moses did say should come:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: That Christ should suffer, and that he should be the first that should rise from the dead, and should shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And as he thus spake for himself,
Paul, thou art beside thyself; much learning doth make thee mad.
segments.speaker: Festus

segments.text: But he said,
I am not mad, most noble Festus; but speak forth the words of truth and soberness.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the king knoweth of these things, before whom also I speak freely: for I am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him; for this thing was not done in a corner.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian.
segments.speaker: Agrippa

segments.text: I would to God, that not only thou, but also all that hear me this day, were both almost, and altogether such as I am, except these bonds.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when he had thus spoken, the king rose up, and the governor, and Bernice, and they that sat with them:
And when they were gone aside, they talked between themselves, saying,
This man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then said Agrippa unto Festus,
This man might have been set at liberty, if he had not appealed unto Cæsar.
segments.speaker: Agrippa
